# ABI Documentation

This directory contains both **maintained** (hand-written) documentation and
**auto-generated** API references. The two categories have different editing
policies: maintained docs are edited directly, while generated docs are
overwritten by the build system and must never be hand-edited.

---

## Maintained Docs

These files are written and updated by contributors. Edit them directly.

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `README.md` | This guide |
| `ABI_WDBX_ARCHITECTURE.md` | WDBX vector database design and storage layout |
| `ZIG_MACOS_LINKER_RESEARCH.md` | Darwin 25+ linker bypass research and workarounds |

---

## Generated API Reference (`api/`)

Created by `zig build gendocs`. **Do not edit these files directly** -- they
are regenerated from source-code comments and will be overwritten.

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `index.md` | Top-level API reference landing page |
| `acp.md` | Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) HTTP server |
| `compute.md` | Distributed mesh networking and multi-GPU orchestration |
| `config.md` | Unified configuration system |
| `connectors.md` | Connector configuration loaders and auth helpers |
| `coverage.md` | Per-module documentation coverage of public symbols |
| `desktop.md` | Native desktop UI extensions and OS integrations |
| `documents.md` | Zero-dependency parsers for complex file formats |
| `errors.md` | Composable error hierarchy for framework operations |
| `feature_catalog.md` | Canonical feature catalog with flag mappings |
| `framework.md` | Framework orchestration and builder pattern |
| `ha.md` | High-availability features for production deployments |
| `lsp.md` | LSP (ZLS) service module |
| `mcp.md` | Model Context Protocol JSON-RPC 2.0 server |
| `platform.md` | OS, architecture, and capability detection |
| `registry.md` | Plugin registry for feature management |
| `runtime.md` | Always-on core runtime infrastructure |
| `shared.md` | Common utilities and cross-cutting concerns |
| `simd.md` | SIMD vector operations and submodule re-exports |
| `tasks.md` | Unified task tracking (personal, roadmap, milestones) |
| `v1.md` | v1 compatibility surface (removed) |

---

## Generated Data (`data/`)

Structured data exports consumed by the web documentation UI. These files are
also regenerated by `zig build gendocs` and should not be hand-edited.

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `commands.zon` | CLI command definitions (names, descriptions, subcommands, aliases) |
| `features.zon` | Feature catalog entries (compile flags, module paths, parent relationships) |
| `guides.zon` | Guide metadata (slugs, titles, sections, ordering) for the web UI sidebar |
| `modules.zon` | Full module tree with public symbols, used for search and cross-referencing |
| `plans.zon` | Roadmap plan entries (now/next/later priorities) |
| `roadmap.zon` | Execution roadmap milestones and status tracking |
| `docs_engine.wasm` | Compiled WASM search/filter engine for the web UI |
| `docs_engine.component.wasm` | WASM component-model variant of the search engine |
| `docs_engine.wit` | WIT interface definition for the WASM docs engine |

---

## Web Documentation UI

The files `index.html`, `index.js`, and `index.css` provide a local
documentation browser. Open `docs/index.html` in any browser to browse API
references, guides, and data files with search and sidebar navigation.

The web UI loads `.zon` data files from `data/` and renders Markdown from
`api/` client-side.

---

## How to Regenerate

**Linux / CI:**

```bash
zig build gendocs
```

**Darwin 25+ (macOS):**

```bash
./tools/scripts/run_build.sh gendocs
```

> On Darwin 25+ the standard `zig build` fails with linker errors. Use the
> wrapper script which bypasses linking. See `ZIG_MACOS_LINKER_RESEARCH.md`
> for background.

**Check mode** (verify docs are up-to-date without writing):

```bash
zig build gendocs -- --check --no-wasm --untracked-md
```

The generator reads source code, templates from `tools/gendocs/templates/`,
and the site map at `tools/gendocs/site_map.zig` to produce all output files
under `docs/`.

---

## Markdown Allowlist Policy

The repository `.gitignore` blocks **all** `*.md` files by default. Only
files that appear in the allowlist are tracked by Git. This prevents
accidental commits of scratch notes or generated Markdown from third-party
tooling.

Current allowlisted paths under `docs/`:

```gitignore
!/docs/README.md
!/docs/ABI_WDBX_ARCHITECTURE.md
!/docs/ZIG_MACOS_LINKER_RESEARCH.md
!/docs/plans/*.md
!/docs/api/*.md
```

If you add a new Markdown file under `docs/`, you **must** also add a
negation rule to `.gitignore` or Git will silently ignore the file.

---

## Adding New Documentation Pages

To add a new generated documentation page:

1. **Create a template** in `tools/gendocs/templates/docs/` following the
   naming convention `<slug>.md.tpl`.

2. **Register the page** in `tools/gendocs/site_map.zig` by adding a new
   `GuideSpec` entry with slug, title, description, section, order,
   permalink, and template path.

3. **Allowlist the output** in `.gitignore`. Generated pages land in
   `docs/api/` (already covered by `!/docs/api/*.md`). If your page goes
   elsewhere under `docs/`, add a negation rule.

4. **Regenerate** by running `zig build gendocs` (or the Darwin wrapper).

To add a new hand-maintained document:

1. Create the `.md` file under `docs/`.
2. Add a `!/docs/<filename>.md` negation rule to `.gitignore`.
3. Update the "Maintained Docs" table in this README.

---
